macro_segments.title: Rav Nachman's Ruling: Direct Observation of Shechita and the Role of Rov Metzuyin

micro_segments.title: Introduction: Picking Up from Daf 12 — Who Can Perform Shechita and When Is It Assumed Correct
Rav Nachman's Ruling: Direct Observation Makes Shechita Valid; Absence of Observation Invalidates It
Gemara's Question: What Is the Case — Known Expert, Known Non-Expert, or Unknown?
Resolution: The Case Is One Where We Don't Know If the Shochet Is Expert — Why Not Rely on Rov Metzuyin?
Baraita Proof: Found a Slaughtered Chicken in the Market — Chazakato Shechuta, We Assume It Was Done Correctly
Discussion: Rov Overrides Chezkat Issur of the Animal; Distinction Between Chicken and Large Animals
Gemara's Answer: The Case Is Actually When We Know He Doesn't Know — He Did One Siman Correctly and You Left
Analysis: Even Correct Start Does Not Guarantee Correct Finish If Shochet Doesn't Know the Laws
Discussion of When We Rely on Rov vs. When We Set Standards — Regulatory Framework of Shechita

macro_segments.title: Shaliach for Shechita and Terumah: Chazakah Shaliach Oseh Shlichuto and Rov Metzuyin

micro_segments.title: Rav Dimi bar Yosef Asks Rav Nachman: Sent Messenger to Slaughter and Found It Already Done — Is It Valid?
Parallel Case of Terumah: Sent Messenger to Separate Terumah and Found It Done — Is It Valid?
The Challenge: Contradiction — If We Assume Shaliach Acts (Chazakah Shaliach Oseh Shlichuto), Both Should Be Valid; If Not, Both Should Be Invalid
Rav Nachman's Answer: Ein Chazakah Shaliach Oseh Shlichuto — But Shechita Is Valid via Rov Metzuyin Regardless of Who Did It
Distinction: Terumah by a Random Person Is Torem Shelo Mida'at and Therefore Invalid
Discussion: Can Blanket Authorization Be Given for Terumah? Chazakah Shaliach and Specific vs. General Agency
Relevance of Chazakah Shaliach Oseh Shlichuto to Gittin and Practical Halachic Contexts

macro_segments.title: Tannaitic Debate: Is Rov Metzuyin Etzel Shechita Mumchin a Universal Principle?

micro_segments.title: Gemara Introduces the Question: Is Rov Metzuyin Etzel Shechita Mumchin a Matter of Tannaitic Dispute?
Baraita: Lost Kid Goats or Chickens Found Slaughtered — Rabbi Yehuda Forbids, Rabbi Chanina Son of Rabbi Yose HaGlili Permits
Rebbi's Ruling: Rabbi Yehuda Seems Right When Found in Garbage Dump; Rabbi Chanina Seems Right When Found in House
Gemara's First Reading: The Dispute Is Whether We Say Rov Metzuyin Etzel Shechita Mumchin
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak's Alternative: Everyone Agrees on Rov Metzuyin; Dispute Is Only About the Private Garbage Dump
The Logical Basis of the Dispute: Would a Person Leave a Neveilah in a Private Garbage Dump or Not?
Rebbi's Statement Re-examined: Apparent Contradiction Resolved — Rebbi Is Defining the Extremes That Are Not Disputed
Summary: Rov Metzuyin Etzel Shechita Mumchin Is Accepted; Review of All Requirements Before Permitting Shechita

macro_segments.title: Cheresh Shoteh veKatan: Prohibition to Give Them Animals to Slaughter and the Question of Kavanah

micro_segments.title: Mishnah Resumed: Cheresh Shoteh veKatan May Not Slaughter Lest They Ruin the Shechita
Rava's Inference: The Mishnah's Phrasing 'Lest They Will Ruin' Means We Do Not Even Give Them Animals Lechatchila
Rashi's Explanation: Even With Observers Present, One Should Not Give Them Animals to Slaughter
Rabbenu Tam's Extension: Even If Intended for Non-Kosher Use or Dog Food, Still Prohibited Due to Ma'arit Ayin Concerns
Mishnah Continues: If They Did Slaughter Under Observation, It Is Kosher Bedieved — Who Is the Tanna?
Why Cheresh Shoteh veKatan Are Not Categorically Excluded: Shechita Is a Physical Act, Not a Status-Changing Act Requiring Da'at
Rava Cites Rabbi Natan via Oshaiya Zeira: Throwing a Knife That Incidentally Performs a Valid Shechita — Rabbi Natan Validates, Chachamim Invalidate
The Halacha Is Ruled Like Rabbi Natan; Gemara Addresses the Requirement of Molich uMeivi
Deep Analysis: Two Types of Kavanah — Da'at for Status-Changing Acts vs. Kavanah for Physical Acts; Shechita and Mitasek
The Central Question: Is Shechita Defined Only by the Physical Act or Must It Be Formally Intended as a Ma'aseh Shechita?
Discussion: Automated Shechita and Koach Gavra — Human Agency as a Separate Requirement from Kavanah

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Question: Does a Katan Have Halachically Relevant Machshava?

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Chiya bar Abba Reports Rabbi Yochanan's Question: Does a Katan Have Machshava — Halachically Relevant Intention?
Rabbi Ami Challenges: Why Ask About Machshava? Ask About Ma'aseh — We Already Know From the Mishna They Have Acts
Mishna Cited: Tinokot Who Hollowed Out Gourds for Use as Vessels — Their Act Is Halachically Significant, Their Thought Alone Is Not
Implicit Tension With Earlier Sugya: If Katan's Acts Are Meaningful, Why Assume They Lack Kavanah for Shechita?
Preview of Tomorrow: The Gemara Will Clarify the Exact Question and How It Resolves

topical_tags.term: Rabbi Yehuda vs. Rabbi Chanina son of Rabbi Yose HaGlili
Rebbi's ruling on garbage dump vs. house
